text: The patient underwent uniportal left videothoracoscopy.
Exploration disclosed a well-defined cystic lesion without lung or closer structures infiltration.
Needle puncture of the cystic wall confirmed the absence of retained blood in the cyst and allowed the gradual deflation of the lesion, thus allowing an effective grasp without any damage.
The cyst was then incised and a crystal-clear fluid of watery consistence, as first described by Greenfield and colleagues in 1943, was drained.
Fluid specimen was collected for cytologic and microbiologic examination.
The cyst was properly inspected and then a wide fenestration was performed by 23 mm ultrasonic shears to obtain an histologic specimen and to avoid cyst recurrence.
Phrenic nerve course was carefully observed and respected to avoid phrenic injuries.
Postoperative course was uneventful and patient was discharged on postoperative day 3 in good clinical conditions.
